source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: Editorial: Special Issue on Topics in Semiconductors, Integrated Circuits, Sensors
paper.abstract: Editorial for CAS 2024 (47th International Semiconductor Conference) special issue. 10 extended contributions from best papers.

verdict.summary: Editorial introduction - not a research paper.

papers.provenance: Editorial for CAS 2024 special issue. No analysis required.

paper.title: Measurement of Electromagnetic Feedthrough in Spin Wave Based Microwave Multiport Devices
paper.abstract: 3-input/1-output spin wave majority gate on YIG. Electromagnetic feedthrough shielding. S-parameter measurements at 8 GHz. GGG substrate characterization.

verdict.summary: RF device characterization with fabrication and measurements. Permittivity extraction equations are standard and necessary for material characterization.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (RF device design)

### Necessity Test
S-parameter equations, permittivity extraction: Standard RF engineering.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: Inclusion of Mechanical Stress Effects in a Compact MOSFET Model
paper.abstract: Mechanical stress effects on MOSFET parameters. Piezo coefficient tensors. EKV model integration. 10% error margin validation.

verdict.summary: Device physics modeling. Stress tensor equations (Eq. 1) are necessary for piezo-coefficient analysis. Standard semiconductor device formalism.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Device modeling)

### Necessity Test
Stress equations, tensor analysis: Necessary for piezo effects.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: Circuit Techniques for Enhancing Output Current Accuracy in Floating Gate Drivers
paper.abstract: Gate driver current compensation techniques. 0.18um BCD technology. Error reduced from 25% to 2%. Motor control application.

verdict.summary: IC design with silicon validation. VGS equation (Eq. 1) is standard for MOSFET analysis. Experimental results confirm simulations.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (IC design)

### Necessity Test
MOSFET equations: Standard analog IC design.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: The Force Extraction Concept with Application to Power IGBTs
paper.abstract: Force Extraction for IGBT turn-OFF speed. Extraction plug and device. 40% turn-OFF energy reduction. 98% efficiency at 25 kHz. Three-phase inverter validation.

verdict.summary: Novel power device concept with TCAD validation. Device physics equations are necessary for IGBT analysis. Practical application demonstrated.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3-4 (Power device innovation)

### Necessity Test
Device physics equations: Necessary for IGBT analysis.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: Separation of Different Orbital Angular Momentum Photon States with Log-pol Transformation
paper.abstract: Log-polar transformation for OAM sorting. Four-level discrete surface approximation. Numerical and experimental validation. Reduced manufacturing overhead.

verdict.summary: Nanophotonics with theoretical and experimental validation. Coordinate transformation equations (Eq. 1-2) are necessary for log-pol optics. Fourier analysis appropriate.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Nanophotonics)

### Necessity Test
Log-pol transformation equations: Necessary for optics.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: Impact of Scaling and Interface Roughness on Drain Current in Nanosheet and Nanowire FETs: A 3D Monte Carlo Analysis
paper.abstract: 3D Monte Carlo simulation of NS and NW FETs. Schrodinger equation quantum corrections. Sub-3nm CMOS technology. Gate length and interface roughness effects.

verdict.summary: Advanced device simulation. Schrodinger equation, Monte Carlo methodology are inherently mathematical. Heavy formalism appropriate for Level 4 device physics.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 4 (Advanced device physics)

### Necessity Test
Quantum corrections, MC simulation: Inherently formal.

**Verdict:** Formalism required and appropriate.

paper.title: Design Space Exploration of Current-Starved Ring Oscillators Based on Graphene Nanoribbons
paper.abstract: GNR-based ring oscillator. 553.8x power reduction vs 7nm FinFET. 88.4 GHz at 0.2V. Quantum capacitance modeling. Design space exploration.

verdict.summary: Novel graphene device design. Conductance and quantum capacitance equations are necessary for GNR modeling. Appropriate for emerging nanoelectronics.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3-4 (Emerging nanoelectronics)

### Necessity Test
Quantum capacitance, conductance modeling: Necessary.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: An Area-Optimized Digitally-Assisted DAC Employing a Matrix-Based DEM Scheme
paper.abstract: 9-bit segmented DAC with DEM. 180nm CMOS. THD -79dB, SFDR 79dB. 8x SFDR improvement, 16x THD improvement over non-DEM.

verdict.summary: Mixed-signal IC design. DAC output equations (Eq. 1) and INL/DNL analysis are standard for converter design. DEM methodology well-justified.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Mixed-signal IC design)

### Necessity Test
DAC equations, DNL/INL: Standard converter analysis.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

paper.title: First and Second Order Digital Circuits with Neuronal Models under Pulses Train Stimulus
paper.abstract: Neuronal models for flip-flop circuits. RS latch, JK latch, D flip-flop, T latch implementations. Pulse train envelope as logic function. Alpha-motoneuron pair simulation.

verdict.summary: Neuromorphic circuit modeling. Circuit schematics and simulation methodology are standard for neuromorphic computing. Minimal formal equations.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 2-3 (Neuromorphic circuits)

### Necessity Test
Minimal formalism - circuit simulation approach.

**Verdict:** Appropriate for application.

paper.title: GaN Bootstrapped Logic Gates Analytical Modeling and Design Insights
paper.abstract: GaN bootstrapped logic gate analysis. Static and dynamic parameter equations. 23.9% max error for static, 33.3% for dynamic. NAND/NOR implementations.

verdict.summary: Power electronics logic design. Analytical equations for GaN logic gates are necessary for design methodology. Validation against simulations.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Level:** 3 (Power electronics logic)

### Necessity Test
Device equations, timing analysis: Necessary for IC design.

**Verdict:** Domain-appropriate formalism.

batch_notes: Volume 28 Issue 2 (2025) - CAS 2024 Special Issue analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **No formalism theater detected.** Strong semiconductor/electronics focus: spin wave devices, MOSFET stress modeling, gate drivers, IGBT optimization, nanophotonics, Monte Carlo device simulation, GNR oscillators, DAC design, neuromorphic circuits, GaN logic. All formalism is domain-appropriate for Level 3-4 electronics/semiconductor research. Final: 0 CRITICAL, 0 LIMITED, 7 ADEQUATE, 3 STRONG (excluding editorial).
